title: NYS DOT Transportation Alternatives Grant -- Westside Pedestrian and Bicycle Safety
summary: DPW engineer Neil presents item 13: NYS DOT Transportation Alternatives grant application for pedestrian and bicycle safety improvements in the Westside/Skunk City area, leveraging existing SMTC studies. Council asks about RFP status and vendor selection; Neil confirms no RFPs yet and will return for bonding authorization. Council emphasizes early vendor selection planning.
raw_content:
[00:06:52] number 13 is
[00:06:55] uh related to a grant opportunity with
[00:06:57] New York State DOT. This is a
[00:06:59] transportation alternatives grant. We've
[00:07:01] been pretty successful with these over
[00:07:02] the past few years and these
[00:07:06] typically cover pedestrian and bicycle
[00:07:08] safety improvements. Um we're working on
[00:07:10] the application now, but we're here for
[00:07:14] um your approval first before we do
[00:07:16] anything.
[00:07:18] >> Pedestrian. Okay. All right. Any
[00:07:20] questions?
[00:07:23] >> Thank you, Neil.
[00:07:24] >> Thank you, Neil. Any more detail at a
[00:07:26] granular level in terms of what you'd
[00:07:28] want to do with this money? So for
[00:07:30] these, we typically utilize studies that
[00:07:33] have already happened from SMTC.
[00:07:35] This area we're looking at near Westside
[00:07:38] Skunk City. Uh there's some really great
[00:07:42] studies that have come out in the past
[00:07:43] few years from our friends at the MO. So
[00:07:46] we're going to repackage that and use
[00:07:47] it. Um as we get further along, we'll be
[00:07:51] back for authorization as it relates to
[00:07:53] any bonding or anything like that
[00:07:55] >> agreement. Yep. Right.
[00:07:56] >> Okay. Thank you.
[00:07:57] >> Yep. Thank you.
[00:07:58] >> No.
[00:07:59] By then, we should be able to have rooms
[00:08:01] to maybe be specific as to what we want
[00:08:05] to engage this money on, right?
[00:08:07] >> Yes.
[00:08:07] >> Okay.
[00:08:08] >> Any RFPs that we should know about?
[00:08:10] >> RFPs? None yet. Um
[00:08:12] >> Okay.
[00:08:13] >> Any specific here?
[00:08:15] >> Feel free to reach out. Always.
[00:08:17] >> Just wanted to make sure that if we were
[00:08:19] writing a grant that involved vendor
[00:08:20] selection that we have that done now
[00:08:22] versus later on.
[00:08:24] >> Yeah. So, we're not there yet, but of
[00:08:26] course, we'll obviously come to you guys
[00:08:28] for approval.
[00:08:30] >> Thank you, Neil.
processing_notes: SMTC = Syracuse Metropolitan Transportation Council. No dollar amounts specified yet. Westside/Skunk City area targeted for pedestrian/bicycle improvements.
Segment 3 of 14 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 4th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: SU -> Syracuse University
